page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192-lssd is a compute-intensive server configured with 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, this instance features a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. The system is equipped with 720 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU.

Local storage is a key feature of this configuration, which bundles 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across 32 drives. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and Passmark database operations. Redis SET operations reach 329.28 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 173.18 GB/sec.

While the server excels in raw compute, memory bandwidth, and local storage throughput, it exhibits a performance tradeoff in LLM text generation. While prompt processing speeds for small, medium, and large models rank in the top 25%, text generation speeds for small and large models fall into the middle 50% tier due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware. Consequently, this server is optimized for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, cryptographic tasks, and parallel data processing workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with a 2.3 GHz clock speed and 504 MB L3 cache. It includes 720 GB of RAM and 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by Redis SET benchmarks achieving 329.28 million operations per second. It supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for accelerated mathematical computations. However, without dedicated GPUs, its LLM text generation performance remains average, making it better suited for high-throughput database hosting, software compilation, cryptographic processing, and large-scale web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192-lssd features 192 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192-lssd is a 192-vCPU server with 720 GB RAM and 12,599 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for high-throughput database tasks.
tagline: A high-density compute and local NVMe storage platform delivering top-tier database throughput and multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with 504 MB L3 cache.
720 GB of system memory paired with 12,599 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier performance in database operations, multi-core processing, and memory bandwidth.
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets.
Optimized for database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
